proposed_from.topic: Exponential-tail, dependency-adaptive nonasymptotic confidence interval for a design-based causal exposure effect under general network interference: for the Horvitz-Thompson exposure estimator theta_hat of an exposure contrast theta=(1/n)sum_i[Y_i(a)-Y_i(b)] under KNOWN Bernoulli assignment with a neighborhood-dependent exposure mapping c_i=g(Z_{N(i)}) and known exposure propensities pi_i(.)>=pi_exp, construct a Holder-normalized fractional-coloring Bernstein e-value over an exact b-fold coloring of the exposure-dependency graph D (i~j iff N(i) cap N(j) nonempty), giving a finite-sample CI |theta_hat-theta| <= sqrt(2 chi_f(D) Vbar log(2/alpha)/n) + chi_f(D) log(2/alpha)/(3 n pi_exp) with observable propensity-only envelope Vbar=n^{-1}sum_i[1/pi_i(a)+1/pi_i(b)], whose half-width tracks the effective independent-exposure count n/chi_f(D) with log(1/alpha) tails -- the first exponential-tail, chi_f-adaptive, general-network finite-sample CI for exposure-HT inference (improving Aronow-Samii's Chebyshev CI), the network-interference lift of the negative-dependence effective-sample-size idea of Sandoval-Balakrishnan-Feller-Jordan-Waudby-Smith 2601.11744
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The nearby downgraded exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt proposal seeks a dependency-CLT and reviewer feedback explicitly asks for a finite-sample inequality with meaningful sharpness. The cited upper interval is supporting machinery; the distinct kernel is the fully specified hard-submodel question of uniform confidence length, which neither the banked polynomial CLT nor its completion-envelope discussion characterizes.","published_axis":"PelekisRamonWang2017 plus AronowSamii2017 supplies the supporting finite-sample chi_f Bernstein interval. TchetgenTchetgenVanderWeele2012 and RigdonHudgens2015 provide finite-sample and exact binary partial-interference intervals, respectively, but no confidence-length lower bound for repeated all-treated/all-control exposures. SandovalEtAl2026 supplies the closest lower-bound architecture only for ordinary randomized experiments. The strict gap is a square-root confidence-length lower bound over a fixed-potential-outcome network exposure submodel with overlapping neighborhoods and an explicitly fixed rare-exposure probability."}
seed_list: Clique-block minimax frontier: determine whether n/chi_f(D_n) is the unavoidable finite-population effective exposure count for all design-valid intervals.
Radius-adaptive ANI interval: balance a deterministic distance-decay bias against a simultaneous fractional-color Bernstein penalty over exposure radii.
Design the randomization: minimize a graph-and-propensity Bernstein-width criterion and certify its relation to minimax confidence length.
Variance-adaptive exposure e-value: combine an estimable conservative quadratic variance bound with fractional-color betting despite zero joint exposure probabilities.
Sequential exposure confidence sequence: construct a predictable fractional-color e-process under repeated network-experiment monitoring.
Weighted fractional-cover interval: characterize the optimal vertex-weighted color cover that replaces global chi_f(D_n)Vbar_n under heterogeneous exposure propensities.
Exposure-mapping coarsening frontier: characterize how coarsening c_i trades target resolution against positivity and finite-sample graph width.
Conflict-graph design lower bound: compare spectral conflict-graph variance design with fractional-color exponential-width design on a common exposure target.

seed_details.one_line: Clique-block minimax frontier: determine whether n/chi_f(D_n) is the unavoidable finite-population effective exposure count for all design-valid intervals.

anchor_paper.author: Sandoval et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Derives effective-sample-size-optimal nonasymptotic intervals in ordinary randomized experiments.
anchor_paper.location: Abstract and stated information-theoretic lower-bound contribution

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Uses a nondegenerate clique-block class, not one hand-tuned potential-outcome schedule.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from SandovalEtAl2026 because a clique block is merely one randomized unit.
seed_details.rebuttal: The reduction must preserve exposure positivity, fixed-potential-outcome contrast geometry, and uniform validity over within-block schedules; those are not in the ordinary-treatment lower bound.
seed_details.burned_reason: The canonical clique-block same-class sandwich is sound but only subfield-level; field-tier salvage requires a genuinely new heterogeneous interval and weighted lower-bound argument.

seed_details.one_line: Radius-adaptive ANI interval: balance a deterministic distance-decay bias against a simultaneous fractional-color Bernstein penalty over exposure radii.

anchor_paper.author: Leung
anchor_paper.claim: Establishes consistency and approximate normality under approximate neighborhood interference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The target is a finite-sample critical-radius rule, not a routine ANI asymptotic extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: Belloni et al.'s neighborhood adaptation already solves radius choice.
seed_details.rebuttal: Existing adaptive procedures target estimation and asymptotic normality, not uniform design-based exponential coverage with truncation bias.

seed_details.one_line: Design the randomization: minimize a graph-and-propensity Bernstein-width criterion and certify its relation to minimax confidence length.

anchor_paper.author: Kandiros et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Constructs conflict-graph designs with spectral variance rates and asymptotic intervals.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The decision variable is the randomization law and a certificate is required.
seed_details.referee_objection: It merely substitutes chi_f for the conflict-graph eigenvalue.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objective couples exposure positivity and dependence and needs a confidence-length comparison, neither supplied by a variance-rate substitution.

seed_details.one_line: Variance-adaptive exposure e-value: combine an estimable conservative quadratic variance bound with fractional-color betting despite zero joint exposure probabilities.

anchor_paper.author: Harshaw et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Characterizes minimally conservative estimable quadratic variance bounds under interference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The shape is self-normalized inference, not variance-estimator optimization alone.
seed_details.referee_objection: A conservative variance estimator cannot control an exponential MGF.
seed_details.rebuttal: That is the substantive obstacle; a predictable domination theorem is needed and is not implied by expectation conservativeness.

seed_details.one_line: Sequential exposure confidence sequence: construct a predictable fractional-color e-process under repeated network-experiment monitoring.

anchor_paper.author: Ham et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Develops design-based confidence sequences for sequential causal experiments.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The claimed object is a test supermartingale, not a stitched reuse of a terminal tail bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: Multiply fixed-time e-values across waves and apply Ville.
seed_details.rebuttal: A single network experiment reveals overlapping exposures within a look, so predictable factorization under the monitoring filtration is the unresolved step.

seed_details.one_line: Weighted fractional-cover interval: characterize the optimal vertex-weighted color cover that replaces global chi_f(D_n)Vbar_n under heterogeneous exposure propensities.

anchor_paper.author: Pelekis et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Proves a b-fold-color Hölder inequality for dependency graphs.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The focal object is an optimally weighted fractional cover, not an algebraic restatement of chi_f.
seed_details.referee_objection: Weighted Hölder immediately gives the answer.
seed_details.rebuttal: The required optimization must retain a common exponential tilt and yield an invertible causal interval; no cited result characterizes that tradeoff.

seed_details.one_line: Exposure-mapping coarsening frontier: characterize how coarsening c_i trades target resolution against positivity and finite-sample graph width.

anchor_paper.author: Aronow and Samii
anchor_paper.claim: Allows the analyst to specify exposure mappings under arbitrary known interference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Requires a derived coarsening frontier rather than a recommendation to use simpler exposures.
seed_details.referee_objection: A coarser map changes the estimand, so there is no comparison.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed object compares an explicit nested family of scientifically interpretable contrasts and identifies the exact width-resolution frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Conflict-graph design lower bound: compare spectral conflict-graph variance design with fractional-color exponential-width design on a common exposure target.

anchor_paper.author: Kandiros et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Uses an estimand-specific conflict graph to construct a modified HT estimator with spectral variance rate.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Requires an observable approximation certificate or a counterexample, not a change of graph functional.
seed_details.referee_objection: Spectral and chromatic measures are incomparable, making the proposal untestable.
seed_details.rebuttal: That incomparability is testable through graph families and a joint lower-bound criterion; it is the named frontier rather than a defect.

proposed_from.literature_map:
AronowSamii2017 frames arbitrary known interference through a design, an exposure mapping, and an exposure-effect estimand; it gives HT unbiasedness, conservative variance estimation, and local-dependence asymptotics.
RigdonHudgens2015 gives finite-sample intervals in structured partial interference, not arbitrary neighborhood-overlap graphs.
PelekisRamonWang2017 proves the exact b-fold-color Hölder inequality required to normalize an exposure-summand MGF by chi_f.
Janson2004 derives strong deviations by partitioning partly dependent sums into independent pieces.
SandovalEtAl2026 closes an effective-sample-size gap and supplies lower-bound logic for ordinary randomized experiments, not exposure-graph dependence.
Leung2022 replaces hard neighborhood cutoffs with approximate neighborhood interference and uses asymptotic network-HAC inference.
KandirosEtAl2024 chooses graph-aware designs and obtains spectral variance rates with asymptotic intervals.
HarshawMiddletonSavje2026 proves that design variance is generally nonestimable under interference and optimizes conservative quadratic bounds.
HamEtAl2022 develops design-based confidence sequences but not exposure-graph e-processes.
The settled part is the fractional-color upper-bound route once local measurability is established; the unsettled part is minimax necessity of the resulting chi_f loss for arbitrary valid intervals.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The claimed frontier is conditional on the balanced clique-block submodel and its stated moderate-deviation regime; it does not assert optimality for arbitrary network graphs or for the Bernstein linear term.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: A positive answer establishes necessity of the square-root r2^r/n scale only for uniformly valid intervals over mathcal M_{n,r}^{block} in r 2^r log(1/alpha)<=c_0 n.

banked_reason: Math review PASS and positioning review PASS, but the independent novelty tier was subfield below the requested field floor.

seeds_burned.one_liner: Clique-block minimax frontier: determine whether n/chi_f(D_n) is the unavoidable finite-population effective exposure count for all design-valid intervals.
seeds_burned.reason: The canonical clique-block same-class sandwich is sound but only subfield-level; field-tier salvage requires a genuinely new heterogeneous interval and weighted lower-bound argument.
